title: Calamagrostis vaseyi
taxonomy: Calamagrostis vaseyi is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Calamagrostis, which is a group of perennial grasses. The genus Calamagrostis is further classified under the subfamily Bambusoideae, which includes bamboo-like grasses. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its growth habit and leaf structure. Calamagrostis vaseyi is native to North America, where it can be found growing in various habitats.

intro: Calamagrostis vaseyi, also known as Vasey's grass or Vasey's fescue, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis vaseyi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Calamagrostis vaseyi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into cough syrups and expectorant formulas to relieve respiratory symptoms.
